response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web software developer with over 30 years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer software (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, DLang). The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep-learning workload optimization for AI accelerators — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His mention of 'AI-agent infrastructure' refers to software-level agentic tooling (MCP frameworks), not hardware or accelerator performance work. There is no meaningful overlap with the core technical requirements of this position.
strengths: 30+ years of continuous professional software development demonstrates sustained technical growth and adaptability
Some exposure to low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second) shows awareness of performance constraints
Contributions to AI-adjacent tooling (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) indicate familiarity with the AI ecosystem at the software layer
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a core listed requirement for this role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or deep neural network training/inference optimization
Entire career is web and application-layer software; no hardware-software co-design, chip architecture, or AI accelerator experience is present
